name: gitlab_runner
explanation: GitLab Runner's prometheus listener (default `:9252/metrics`) is unauthenticated and could be discovered with a generic OpenMetrics port-scan. However the check also requires `gitlab_url` (a separate connectivity probe to the GitLab master, sometimes with `?token=<TOKEN>` for CE deployments) and `allowed_metrics` (an init_config metric-allowlist with no usable default — the spec example is the canonical list but it's marked required). Discovery layer must (a) probe the runner's prometheus port and (b) inject a default allowlist; the gitlab_url field is best-effort and tokens are out-of-band.
notes: Three required fields, two of them deployment-specific (gitlab_url, allowed_metrics). The prometheus endpoint itself is generic, but the check's broader contract requires custom logic to populate the other fields, including the GitLab master URL which lives on a different host.
